title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit · Independent Query Trial
summary: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.
Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Audit Provenance
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-audit-provenance-pg0u2n" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; parameterization, authorization, minimization, or audit evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-audit-provenance-pg0u2n
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE opened_at >= :retention_boundary
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
